title: Home - Reserve Bank of New Zealand - Te Pūtea Matua
content:
It looks like the browser you're using doesn’t work well with our website. For a better experience, please update to the latest version of Chrome , Edge , Firefox or Safari .
We are Aotearoa New Zealand's central bank

## The Reserve Bank of New Zealand – Te Pūtea Matua
We aim to keep prices stable, maintain cash supplies and protect the stability of our financial system.
Updated:
                            
                                2:00pm,  27 May 2026
Next update:

Next update:
                            
                                3:00pm,  21 Jul 2026
Updated: 11 Jun 2026

### Inflation calculator update
The inflation calculator now includes a rent category. Use the calculator to see how rental costs have changed over time.

### 2026 Stress testing scenarios
Our new Bulletin outlines the scenarios for our 2026 stress tests of large and small banks, and the life and health insurance sector.

### July Monetary Policy Review and OCR

### Monetary Policy Statement and OCR September 2026

### Monetary Policy Review and OCR October 2026

### Financial Stability Report November 2026

### Monetary Policy Statement and OCR December 2026

### February 2027 Monetary Policy Review and OCR

### ASB Bank ordered to pay $6.731 million in largest AML/CFT penalty to date
The Reserve Bank of New Zealand – Te Pūtea Matua welcomes the High Court’s decision to impose a civil pecuniary penalty of $6.731 million on ASB Bank Limited.

### RBNZ announces decision on use of the word 'bank'
All New Zealand deposit takers licensed under the DTA will be able to call themselves banks.

### Cash consultation alternate formats and additional information published
Alternate formats of the Keeping Cash Local consultation document and survey – in NZSL, Braille, Easy Read, Audio and Large Print – are now available for disabled people and their communities to have their say.

### RBNZ Governor connects with Hawke's Bay on Monetary Policy
On Friday 29 May, Reserve Bank of New Zealand Governor, Dr Anna Breman, spoke to rural and primary sector leaders in the Hawke’s Bay discussing insights from the May 2026 Monetary Policy Statement.

### Prudential Levy Consultation
As part of the Government’s Budget 2026 the Minister of Finance announced the Government’s intention to introduce a prudential levy under the Reserve Bank of New Zealand Act 2021 (RBNZ Act).
